description: Withanage Kamani Perera Vs. State Mortgage and Investment Bank and Others Withanage Kamani Perera Vs. State Mortgage and Investment Bank and Others View More before Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: Withanage Kamani Perera Vs. State Mortgage and Investment Bank and Others Withanage Kamani Perera Vs. State Mortgage and Investment Bank and Others View More
judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner was an Attorney-at-Law attached to the legal division of the 1st Respondent Bank; was unduly and irrationally denied third extension after completing 57 years of service, which was challenged successfully; according to the judgement, she was reinstated from the date of the judgement. After reinstatement and service of two more years, at retirement she asked for the back- wages and other emoluments, which was denied; challenged the same. Respondents took up the position that there was a break in service; therefore, not entitled to back-wages during which she had not worked until she was reinstated. Held – break of her service was not caused by the Petitioner but by the Respondents; therefore, entitled to the back-wages and other emoluments.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka
